response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with broad experience across web, mobile, and systems programming, but his background is almost entirely JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js with no meaningful exposure to the Java/Kotlin/JVM ecosystem, Spring or enterprise frameworks, or IDE internals. The Java entries on his resume are peripheral and dated (2014–2018), and there is no evidence of JVM depth, Spring experience, or enterprise framework work. His brief stint at National Instruments involved C/C++ for a C IDE, not JVM tooling. This role demands exactly the expertise that is absent from his profile.
strengths: Demonstrated ability to build developer tooling (test runner, assertion library, MCP code generators) showing genuine interest in developer experience
Experience working on large-scale, high-throughput systems (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/second) indicates comfort with complex infrastructure
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record (workshops, conference talks, Selenium courses) aligns with collaboration requirements
concerns: Primary technical stack is TypeScript/Node.js — Java and Kotlin are listed as peripheral skills with no substantial recent production use
No experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework mentioned anywhere in the resume
No IDE internals experience; the NI LabWindows role was C/C++ component work, not JVM-based IDE subsystem development

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
